rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE MAURICIO CARIAS SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. Share of rent and parking payable by Vanessa Carias and Marco Meza are not reflected in the receipts schedule. Trustee alleges Vanessa will bring a petition to request caregivers fees for prior time period, from which the rent and parking will be deducted from. 2. Per supplement filed on 3/17/25, Trustee states that a cleaner goes to Mauricios home twice a week at a daily rate of $125 to do household tasks that Vanessa Carias is unable to complete due to an arm injury. 3. Trustee utilized a TrueLink account for the familys use for Mauricios benefit. ~$25K was used during this 12-month period, and many expenditures are inadequately described / have other issues. For example: a. $474.40 was spent for clothing, but no descriptions are provided for what type of clothing, which is problematic, as Vanessa Carias has made purchases before for her private use. b. $125.44 Best Buy purchase on 12/12/23 without any description. c. $264.78 Target purchase for cleaning supplies d. $229.52 Amazon purchase for fabuloso, towels, dish soap e. $589.74 Amazon purchase for diapers, underpads, wipes, oral care, ointment f. $385.91 Walgreen purchase for diapers, otc medication, under pads $562.72 Comcast disbursement for DOS 1/16/24  2/15/24. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LISA ANNE CHRISTENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet, as ordered on 11/29/23. Letters were issued on 12/7/23. [Prob. C. Sect. 12200.] The I&A filed on 5/7/25 does not appear to have been signed by the administrator. There are initials by the electronic signature. If the daughter is still acting on her mother's behalf, she must file some proof of her authority to do so or the administrator must resign and allow someone else to be appointed as the successor administrator. The decedent was reported as owning real property in the initial petition filed on 9/19/23. No real property has been inventoried and appraised by the probate referee. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLARA WOOTEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/3/25. Examiner's Notes sent on 4/24/25 with the following issues: 1) Petitioner alleges that Decedent died on 2/7/11, but the order for probate indicates that Decedent died on 2/2/11. 2) Petitioner alleges that the petition for letters of administration was filed on 3/12/24, but the petition was filed on 3/21/24. 3) Petitioner alleges that CVCB has a claim of $4,793.93 against Oscar Anthony Wooten Jr.'s interest in the estate; however, the declaration filed by CVCB on 7/18/24 states that the amount is $7,800. 4) A receipt of distribution for $11,000 must be filed by Leatha Wooten. 5) Please allege whether the Franchise Tax Board has been given notice under Probate Code 9202. If notice was given, please allege when notice was sent. Please note that the applicable time period must lapse after notice is properly given prior to the estate closing. 6) The proposed distribution must specify the total interest in the estate (in fractions, after combining interests resulting from postdeceased beneficiaries) and the specific amount of cash that will be distributed to each heir. 7) The body of the petition requests $1,500 as reserve, but the prayer of the petition does not. 8) An assignment was filed on 11/5/24 showing that Petitioner, as power of attorney for Leatha Wooten, assigned $20,000 of her interest to ProbateCash. The power of attorney must be filed showing his authority to assign her interest in this estate. 9) 13100 Affidavits must be filed for Derek Wooten and Luvirie Johnson prior to the granting of the petition. Please note that the original affidavits must be filed in person (not e-filed), attaching certified death certificates and Judicial Council Form DE-300. 10) As part of its decision regarding the reasonableness of extraordinary fees, this court generally requires a showing that statutory fees are not sufficient to compensate for all services performed [Estate of Walker 221 Cal.App. 2d 792]. Please provide a verified statement of ordinary services provided and timesheets. 11) Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motions To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Petitioner Matthew DeRyan's Motion to Change Venue. The Motion to Change Venue is DENIED. The Motion is denied on procedural grounds. The Motion to Change Venue was filed on March 18, 2025. Declaration of Matthew DeRyan Concerning Difficulty Serving Parties was filed on March 21, 2025. It includes several unfiled Proof of Service second pages, indicating papers were served on various people on February 13, 2025 and March 14, 2025. There is no showing on the record that the filed Motion and notice of the hearing date have been served on the necessary parties, including the Trustee of the Living Trust of Gerald DeRyan Trust (see, Probate Code sections 1200 et seq, 17100, and 17203). If the Petitioner feels another county or state would be a more appropriate venue to hear the Petition, he may dismiss the Petition before this court and refile the Petition in that other venue (see, Probate Code sections 17002 and 17005). Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRANK S.Y. CHIN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The petition does not address how this is the proper venue when the trustee resides in Oregon. (3) The correct form of notice for a petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/17/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERAINA L. JOHNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The petition caption is incomplete if the petitioner seeks IAEA authority. (3) No proof of publication of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (4) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (5) Mailed notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (6) If the whereabouts of a person entitled to notice are unknown, the petitioner must first file an ex parte petition to dispense with notice to that person. The ex parte petition must comply with CRC 7.52. (7) Item 3(e)(3) of the petition is incomplete, and no waivers of bond have been filed. (8) Items 5a(3) or (4) and 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (9) No Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (10) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (11) Why is a formal probate proceeding necessary, when the estimated value of the estate is just $15,000 in personal property? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If a formal probate proceeding is really required, the petitioner might wish to hire an attorney.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTELITA B PASILLAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/3/25. Examiner's Notes sent to attorney with the following issues: 1) A written renunciation should be filed by those who are entitled to priority for appointment but who do not desire to act. Where not filed, the petition should indicate the reason [Local Rule 14.24]. 2) Please allege how petitioner is an interested person and what priority he has to serve as administrator. 3) Item 5a is incomplete. 4) Item 6 is marked, but issue are listed in Item 8. 5) Item 7 is marked, but issue are listed in Item 8. 6) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. 7) Amendment filed on 4/25/25 requests bond in the amount of $275K, but no authority has been cited to allow bond less than the full value of the estate. Notice of Hearing has also not been filed on the filing of the amendment. 8) Proof of Publication has not been filed.
